steps.title: Spot the contrasting outcomes
steps.content: The key signal is the opposition between “benefits the collective” (fish example) and “often with unfavorable results” (sandpiper example). These describe the same phenomenon with opposite effects.

steps.title: Select the transition that signals a shift
steps.content:
”Conversely” marks the flip side—showing cascading behavior causing harm where the fish study demonstrated benefit.

The other choices either rephrase the same idea, confirm it, or suggest a cause-effect link—none work when the second example contradicts the first.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
